inclusion_criteria: Human clinical studies (RCTs, nonrandomized interventional, or observational cohorts/registries) that report within-patient pre- vs post-intervention data
Adults (≥18 years) with chronic kidney disease and treatment-resistant hypertension (investigator-defined; e.g., uncontrolled on ≥3 antihypertensive agents or controlled on ≥4)
Intervention: endovascular catheter-based radiofrequency renal denervation of renal sympathetic nerves
Report at least one specified outcome at baseline and ≥1 follow-up: office or ambulatory systolic/diastolic blood pressure, eGFR, serum creatinine, or procedural complication rates
exclusion_criteria: Non-human, pediatric, or non-clinical designs (animal/in vitro studies, reviews, editorials, letters without original patient data)
Populations without CKD or without treatment-resistant hypertension, or mixed cohorts without extractable data for patients with both conditions
Interventions not involving radiofrequency catheter-based renal denervation (e.g., ultrasound-based RDN, surgical sympathectomy, chemical denervation) or pharmacotherapy-only studies
No baseline and follow-up (or change-from-baseline) data for the specified outcomes
screening_criteria.description: Screen for adult clinical studies of endovascular radiofrequency renal denervation in CKD patients with treatment-resistant hypertension that report pre- and post-intervention blood pressure, kidney function, or complication outcomes.

included_articles.title: Renal denervation preserves renal function in patients with chronic kidney disease and resistant hypertension.
[Ultra-long-term follow-up of renal denervation in patients with resistant hypertension and mild chronic kidney disease].
Insight on Efficacy of Renal Artery Denervation for Refractory Hypertension with Chronic Kidney Diseases: A Long-Term Follow-Up of 24-Hour Ambulatory Blood Pressure.
Renal denervation in moderate to severe CKD.
Insights on safety and efficacy of renal artery denervation for uncontrolled-resistant hypertension in a high risk population with chronic kidney disease: first Italian real-world experience.
Renal denervation in patients with end-stage renal disease and resistant hypertension on long-term haemodialysis.
Renal denervation using carbon dioxide renal angiography in patients with uncontrolled hypertension and moderate to severe chronic kidney disease.
